message:
feat(terminal,neuro,render,docs): add terminal brain visualization, NeuroFlow activation snapshots, neural edge rendering, and camera offscreen guard

This commit enhances brain visualization across both terminal and GUI renderers, implements activation snapshots for NeuroFlow brains, fixes critical camera panning bugs that could leave the canvas blank, and expands REST API documentation with practical examples.

Terminal brain visualization (scriptbots-app/src/terminal/mod.rs):
- Added compact ASCII brain activation display in the Insights panel showing real-time neuron firing patterns
- Renders first activation layer using Unicode block characters (█▆▅▃▂▁) mapping activation values 0.0-1.0 to visual intensity
- Takes first 32 columns of the activation grid for space-efficient inline display
- Added BrainLayerView struct to snapshot and cache activation data from agent runtime
- Snapshot now pulls brain_activations from first agent's runtime for real-time visualization
- Added focused_agent_cursor and activation_layer_index fields for future navigation controls (multi-agent brain comparison)
- Brain visualization appears directly below analytics sparklines, providing instant neural feedback during simulation runs

Implementation details:
- Maps activation values to 6-level Unicode intensity: >0.8 = '█', >0.6 = '▆', >0.4 = '▅', >0.2 = '▃', >0.1 = '▂', else '▁'
- BrainLayerView::from_activations extracts first layer from BrainActivations, handling missing data gracefully
- Only renders when brain_activations are available (MLP and NeuroFlow now provide snapshots, others return None)

NeuroFlow brain activation snapshots (scriptbots-brain-neuro/src/lib.rs):
- Implemented BrainRunner::snapshot_activations for NeuroflowBrain, enabling visualization of multi-layer neural networks
- Uses pragmatic serde reflection approach to extract internal layer outputs (y values) since NeuroFlow doesn't expose public getters
- Serializes network to JSON, parses layers array, extracts y field from each layer
- Converts f64 values to f32 and arranges them in square-ish grids (width = sqrt(num_neurons), auto-computed height)
- Layer names follow pattern "nf.layer.{index}" for inspector display
- Returns empty activations if serde reflection fails, ensuring graceful degradation

Why serde reflection:
- NeuroFlow struct fields are not public, so we can't directly access layer outputs
- JSON serialization exposes the full state including hidden layer activations
- This approach works with current NeuroFlow 1.x structure but may need updates if NeuroFlow changes internal layout
- Alternative would be patching NeuroFlow to expose getters, but reflection keeps the codebase decoupled

GUI neural connection rendering (scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs):
- Added paint_activation_edges function to draw connection lines between neurons in activation heatmaps
- Overlays edge canvas on top of activation grid canvas using relative positioning
- Filters edges to only those within the current layer's index space (from/to < width × height)
- Line thickness scales with absolute weight (0.5px base + 1.5px × weight), clamped 0.1-1.0
- Color encodes sign: cyan (rgba 0x22d3ee) for positive weights, pink (rgba 0xf471b5) for negative weights
- Limits to 64 edges per layer to prevent visual clutter and maintain performance
- Lines connect cell centers: (x % cols + 0.5, x / cols + 0.5) in grid coordinates, transformed to pixel space

Edge rendering implementation:
- Uses PathBuilder with stroke width based on connection weight magnitude
- Computes grid cell centers (not corners) for more natural connection visualization
- Alpha channel 0.85 provides semi-transparency so overlapping edges remain distinguishable
- Handles edge cases: empty edge lists, zero dimensions, invalid indices all skip rendering gracefully

Camera offscreen guard and auto-recenter (scriptbots-render/src/lib.rs):
- Fixed critical bug where extreme panning + zoom could push the world render rectangle completely outside the canvas viewport, resulting in a blank screen with no visual recovery
- Added offscreen detection: checks if render rectangle (render_left, render_top, render_right, render_bottom) lies entirely outside view bounds
- When fully offscreen detected, camera auto-recenters on world midpoint (world_size × 0.5) to restore visible content
- Recalculates offsets after recentering to ensure current frame draws correctly
- Follow mode now updates offsets after camera.center_on to reflect position changes

Technical details of offscreen guard:
- View bounds: (origin_x, origin_y) to (origin_x + width_px, origin_y + height_px)
- Render bounds: (offset_x, offset_y) to (offset_x + render_w, offset_y + render_h)
- Fully offscreen condition: render_right < view_left OR render_left > view_right OR render_bottom < view_top OR render_top > view_bottom
- After auto-recenter, recomputes base_scale, render dimensions, padding, and offsets to draw world at proper location
- This prevents the "lost in space" UX where users pan too far and can't find their way back without restarting

Why this matters:
- Users exploring large worlds with high zoom levels could accidentally pan beyond world boundaries
- Previous behavior: blank canvas with no indication of where the world went or how to recover
- New behavior: automatic snap-back to world center when view goes offscreen, maintaining usability
- Especially important for touchpad/trackpad users who can easily overshoot during fast panning gestures

Documentation updates (README.md):
- Added SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_EMOJI environment variable documentation: allows forcing emoji mode on/off with values 1|true|yes|on or 0|false|off|no
- Documented low-power mode behavior: in addition to capping thread count via --low-power flag, the app now reduces OS process priority (Unix: nice +10, Windows: BELOW_NORMAL) to be a better background citizen when running long simulations
- Added REST API examples section showing practical curl commands for common operations:
  * Listing and applying presets (GET /api/presets, POST /api/presets/apply with {"name":"arctic"})
  * Fetching recent events tail (GET /api/events/tail?limit=10)
  * Querying scoreboard for top predators and oldest agents (GET /api/scoreboard?limit=10)
  * Streaming tick updates via SSE (curl -N with Accept: text/event-stream header to /api/ticks/stream)
- Clarified selection API modes: updated POST /api/selection documentation to show modes as set/add/clear (was previously set/add/remove) and documented state values as none|hovered|selected
- All curl examples include jq pipe for pretty JSON output, making them copy-paste ready for developers exploring the REST API

REST API documentation rationale:
- Many users weren't aware of the full REST API surface area beyond basic endpoints
- Concrete curl examples lower the barrier to entry for API exploration and scripting
- SSE streaming example demonstrates real-time integration possibilities for dashboards and monitoring tools
- Preset application example shows how to programmatically switch scenarios without GUI interaction

Integration notes:
- Terminal brain visualization uses the activation snapshot infrastructure added in previous commit (08d2cf7)
- NeuroFlow snapshots integrate seamlessly with existing BrainActivations type, enabling multi-brain-type visualization
- Edge rendering complements existing heatmap visualization, providing connection topology insight alongside activation intensity
- Camera fixes improve overall UX for both regular navigation and follow-mode scenarios
- Documentation improvements help users discover and leverage existing functionality

Testing recommendations:
- Terminal: run with MLP or NeuroFlow brains, observe ASCII brain bar in Insights panel responding to agent behavior
- GUI: open inspector on selected agent, verify activation heatmap shows colored grid with overlaid connection lines
- Camera: zoom in heavily, pan far beyond world boundaries, verify auto-recenter triggers and world reappears
- REST API: test curl examples against running simulation server on port 8088

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -386,6 +386,7 @@ cargo build -p scriptbots-brain-ml --features candle
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_FRAMES` — number of frames to render in headless mode (default 12; max 360).
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_HEADLESS_REPORT` — file path to write a JSON summary from a headless run.
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_MAX_THREADS` — preferred maximum thread budget; core will cap Rayon to min of CPUs and this value (used unless `RAYON_NUM_THREADS` is already set).
+ - `SCRIPTBOTS_TERMINAL_EMOJI` — force emoji mode `1|true|yes|on` or disable with `0|false|off|no`.
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_NEUROFLOW_ENABLED` — `true|false`.
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_NEUROFLOW_HIDDEN` — comma-separated hidden sizes (e.g., `64,32,16`).
 - `SCRIPTBOTS_NEUROFLOW_ACTIVATION` — `tanh|sigmoid|relu`.
@@ -602,6 +603,7 @@ order by bucket;
    - `--profile-steps N` and `--profile-storage-steps N` to run headless micro-benchmarks
    - `--profile-sweep N` and `--auto-tune N` to explore and auto-pick thread/flush settings
    - `--renderer-safe` for a conservative paint path; `--debug-watermark` overlays a diagnostics badge
+ - Low-power mode: in addition to capping threads (`--low-power`), the app lowers OS process priority (Unix niceness +10; Windows BELOW_NORMAL) to be a better background citizen.
 
 ## Tracing & logging
 - Logging uses `tracing` with `RUST_LOG` filters (e.g., `RUST_LOG=info,scriptbots_core=debug`).
@@ -633,7 +635,7 @@ order by bucket;
   - `GET /api/events/tail` → recent events (birth/death/combat) ring buffer
   - `GET /api/scoreboard` → top carnivores and oldest agents at a glance
   - `GET /api/agents/debug` → lightweight agent debug table (filters: ids, diet, selection, brain)
-  - `POST /api/selection` → queue a selection update (modes: set/add/remove; optional state)
+  - `POST /api/selection` → queue a selection update (modes: set/add/clear; optional state: none|hovered|selected)
   - `GET /api/presets` → list scenario presets
   - `POST /api/presets/apply` → apply preset by name
   - `GET /api/config/audit` → recent config patches (audit ring buffer)
@@ -654,6 +656,22 @@ curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/selection \
   -d '{"mode":"add","agent_ids":[4,5],"state":"highlighted"}'
 ```
 
+More examples:
+```bash
+# List and apply a preset via REST
+curl -s http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/presets | jq
+curl -s -X POST http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/presets/apply -H 'content-type: application/json' -d '{"name":"arctic"}' | jq .
+
+# Recent events (tail)
+curl -s 'http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/events/tail?limit=10' | jq .
+
+# Scoreboard (top predators, oldest agents)
+curl -s 'http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/scoreboard?limit=10' | jq .
+
+# SSE tick stream (press Ctrl+C to stop)
+curl -N -H 'Accept: text/event-stream' http://127.0.0.1:8088/api/ticks/stream | sed -n '1,10p'
+```
+

 ```bash
 # 1) Start the app
